content.text: I need to revert a change to my last commit message

What would you like the new commit message to be? The last commit on this branch is:

```
42e6c78 test
```

To change it, I can run `git commit --amend -m "new message"`. What message would you like?
No I want to revert the extra amendment to the commit message where the new entire-checkpoint text was added
Let me check the current full commit message first.
I see — the commit message has `Entire-Checkpoint` lines appended. What should the message be after reverting? Just `test` with no checkpoint lines?
it shouldn't have this one: Entire-Checkpoint: c81467b72ca0

input.command:
git commit --amend -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test

Entire-Checkpoint: ecf782729563
EOF
)"
input.description: Amend commit message to remove the extra checkpoint line

result.output:
[test-gemini 70a873a] test
 Date: Tue Mar 17 15:39:58 2026 -0700
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+)

content.text: Done. The extra `Entire-Checkpoint: c81467b72ca0` line has been removed.
